deadmau5~professional-griefers: Professional Griefers was released as the third single from deadmau5’s sixth studio album (fourth mainstream album) > album title goes here <. It became a moderate success, peaking in the top 30 of the alternative charts in both the US and Canada, and reached 81 on the UK Singles Chart.
machine-head~imperium: About confidence and self-empowerment and not giving in to the naysayers
nine-inch-nails~wish: From 1992’s EP, Broken, which is also known as Halo 5. This song is notable for winning a 1993 Grammy, despite being heavily censored for masochistic themes and rough lyrics.
nine-inch-nails~gave-up: Fading in with an ambient pad, the quick drum loop soon enters, with Reznor’s quiet and filtered vocals soon after. His singing builds in intensity until launching into the pre-chorus, which is considerably louder and guitar-centric, also featuring yelled vocals.
linkin-park~numb: “Numb” is not only one of Linkin Park’s most recognizable and emotionally charged tracks, but also one of their most commercially successful. Written just a week before the band entered the studio, the song quickly became a defining moment for Linkin Park, both musically and emotionally.
linkin-park~papercut: “Papercut” is the opening track on Linkin Park’s debut album, Hybrid Theory. The song combines hip-hop beats, electronic textures, and nu-metal guitar riffs, reflecting the band’s genre-blending signature.
linkin-park~faint: “Faint” stands as one of Linkin Park’s most iconic tracks from their sophomore album, Meteora. The song’s instantly recognizable string-driven intro and powerful delivery have made it a fan favorite and a staple in the band’s live performances.
linkin-park~somewhere-i-belong: Officially released to U.S. radio on March 18, 2003, the highly popular song was often requested on modern rock radio, and reached number one on several music charts.
linkin-park~hit-the-floor: “Hit the Floor” stands as one of Meteora’s more rhythmically aggressive tracks, blending Linkin Park’s signature nu-metal intensity with a hip-hop-inspired backbone.
linkin-park~from-the-inside: “From the Inside” is a hard-hitting Linkin Park track that channels frustration and emotional struggle. It appears as the tenth song on the band’s sophomore album, Meteora, and was released as the fourth single from the record.
deadmau5~maths: Maths was originally intended to be the 12th track on 4x4=12. Although the track was not finished in time.
bring-me-the-horizon~dear-diary: The album POST HUMAN: SURVIVAL HORROR makes references to several video games. In “Dear Diary,” there are multiple allusions to the original Resident Evil.
babymetal~starlight: The first in the trio of songs that the band has referred to as a “Trilogy of Lights”, the heavily djent-inspired and emotional “Starlight” serves as a possible tribute to former guitarist Mikio Fujioka, who tragically died in early 2018 of injuries sustained after falling from an observatory deck while stargazing.
nine-inch-nails~discipline: “Discipline” is a single from Nine Inch Nails and also the fourth track on Halo 27, The Slip. It contains a beat reminiscent of “Only” from With Teeth, and its lyrics seem to have a sado-masochistic overtone.
ozzy-osbourne~let-me-hear-you-scream: The first single off Scream is about yelling and letting out that built-up angst to scream for what you believe in. It is Ozzy’s first release to feature new guitarist Gus G.
linkin-park~in-the-end: “In the End” stands as Linkin Park’s most iconic track, serving as the fourth single from their multi-platinum debut album, Hybrid Theory. The song became a cultural touchstone, blending Mike Shinoda’s rap verses with Chester Bennington’s soaring chorus.
crystal-castles~concrete: “Concrete“ is the ninth song on Crystal Castles' album “Amnesty (I)“. Along with “Enth“ and “Fleece“, it is one of the three songs written by their new vocalist Edith Frances.
queens-of-the-stone-age~first-it-giveth: “First It Giveth” was released as the third and last single off of Songs For The Deaf. This track is recognizable by its crunchy lead guitars and the melodic vocals on the chorus.
linkin-park~with-you: “With You” stands as a pivotal track on Linkin Park’s groundbreaking debut album Hybrid Theory, exemplifying the band’s early fusion of rap-rock, electronic experimentation, and raw emotional intensity.
nine-inch-nails~the-hand-that-feeds: “The Hand That Feeds” is a verbal attack on the United States Government, specifically the Bush Administration and its foreign policy, but can be interpreted as speaking out against abusive authority in general.
linkin-park~a-place-for-my-head: “A Place for My Head” originally rooted in their earliest creative explorations as the band Xero. The ninth track on their debut album Hybrid Theory, the song evolved from a demo titled “Esaul,” named after a friend of the band.
type-o-negative~i-don-t-wanna-be-me: The first single from the 2003 album Life Is Killing Me provides a blistering open to the album. At the time, Peter (Steele, vocals, bass) wasn’t really doing too well, health-wise.
nine-inch-nails~somewhat-damaged: “Somewhat Damaged” is the first track on the Left side of the double disc The Fragile, the third studio album from Nine Inch Nails, released in 1999. It primarily builds using percussive samples, without many melodic elements.
machine-head~aesthetics-of-hate: The third track from Machine Head’s 2007 album The Blackening, and the first single from that album.
nine-inch-nails~march-of-the-pigs: “March of the Pigs”, its single also being known as Halo 7 stands as the fastest NIN song ever recorded (coming it at 269 BPM) and features an aggressive verse followed by a stripped-down piano chorus.
nine-inch-nails~head-like-a-hole: The first song on Pretty Hate Machine and second single from it, “Head Like A Hole” is one of the more popular songs on the album. The single release is also known as Halo 3, and contains 11 tracks, including four versions of the single, plus remixes of “Terrible Lie” and “Down In It” Reznor claims he “wrote it at the last minute as a throwaway”.
linkin-park~points-of-authority-cristal-: “Points of Authority” is the fourth track on Linkin Park’s debut album, Hybrid Theory. The song originated from a 1999 demo titled “Oh No”, released later on the LP Underground X: Demos album.
queens-of-the-stone-age~go-with-the-flow: “Go with the Flow” was released as the second single off of QotSA’s third album Songs for the Deaf. The song is about a woman going from relationship to relationship, ditching when things get serious and leaving behind broken hearts.﻿ It was nominated for “Best Hard Rock Performance” at the 2004 Grammys.
bring-me-the-horizon~kingslayer-feat-bab: “Kingslayer” is an ode to someone who’s “willing to do what’s right even if it’s illegal.” Kingslayer is also a reference to Call of Duty which when you kill the top player on the other team you are awarded the Kingslayer medal.
machine-head~clenching-the-fists-of-diss: An opener that starts with some gentle acoustic picking mixed with garbled sounding radio messages that sound from as if they’re from The Middle East, Clenching The Fists of Dissent is a song that harshly critiques American politics in the post 9/11 world.
linkin-park~don-t-stay: “Don’t Stay” opens Meteora with a burst of frustration and defiance, channeling Linkin Park’s struggle against toxic-influences—whether in personal relationships or the music industry itself.
nine-inch-nails~only: “Only” was the second single released from Halo 19, Nine Inch Nails' fourth album, With Teeth. It was the last song completed during the With Teeth recordings.
pro8l3m~molly: Tekst jest o życiu w wirtualnej rzeczywistości. Sam teledysk odgrywa się na złomowisku, co pokazuje, że ludzie mniej zaczęli interesować się realnym życiem, a bardziej tym wirtualnym, w którym wystawne życie jest dostępne dla każdego.
slayer~jihad: The song portrays the imagined viewpoint of a terrorist who has participated in the September 11, 2001 attacks, concluding with spoken lyrics taken from words left behind by Mohamed Atta.
linkin-park~by-my-self-remix: “By Myself” stands out for its aggressive energy, atmospheric electronic-laced verses, and raw lyrical themes of isolation and internal struggle. The track originated from elements of a demo called “Sad,” and was initially titled “Super Xero” during the band’s early development stages.
puddle-of-mudd~famous: Famous is the first song and single from the album of the same name, from 2007. The song was sent out to radios May 11th the same year, while being released as a single 10 days later.
system-of-a-down~war: In this song, System of a Down denounces the reason behind every war: power struggles, using the Christian Inquisition and modern warfare on the Middle East as examples.
crystal-castles~celestica: One of Crystal Castles (II)’s softer numbers featuring clearly audible lyrics, Celestica touches the themes of disillusionment with society, nihilism and grief.
northlane~bloodline: “Bloodline” is a single that came out along side “Vultures” which both appear on Northlane’s 2019 album “Alien”. The lyrics detail Marcus Bridge’s upbringing under an abusive father and how he managed to get himself out.
the-dillinger-escape-plan~black-bubblegu: This song is about a huge bitch.
queens-of-the-stone-age~you-think-i-ain-: The opening track on Queens of the Stone Age’s third (and arguably most famous) album, Songs for the Deaf. The song begins with the sound of a car starting and a radio turning on, which introduces the use of short radio segments between the album’s songs to tell a loose story about driving from Los Angeles out into the California high desert.
slipknot~left-behind: Track 8 on Iowa, “Left Behind” was the 2nd single release on the album. It was nominated for the 2002 Grammy for Best Metal Performance (won by Tool – Schism).
nine-inch-nails~the-big-come-down: A unique track in the NIN canon, it features a loud and bombastic drum loop with odd percussion sounds, synthesized bass, and an oddly-tuned acoustic guitar riff.
linkin-park~lying-from-you: “Lying from You” is a powerful track from Linkin Park’s second studio album Meteora, serving as the fourth single released from the record. The song features the band’s signature blend of nu-metal and rap, with Chester Bennington’s intense vocals complementing Mike Shinoda’s rap verses.
korn~spike-in-my-veins: Front man Jonathan Davis wrote the track initially with members of the electronic group Noisia while on tour in the Netherlands. Watch the official video here: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FOUlFFPf6ck
bring-me-the-horizon~teardrops: “Teardrops” is the fourth single from POST HUMAN: SURVIVAL HORROR. It talks about kids growing up in today’s age with tech addiction being common place and the problems of it.
slayer~eyes-of-the-insane: The lyrics explore an American soldier’s mental anguish following his return home from the second Gulf War, and are based on an article entitled “Casualty of War” in Texas Monthly magazine.
marilyn-manson~deep-six: Lyrics inspiration: The close relation between sex and violence, between good news and bad news, helps to explain the compulsion of the admen to dunk all their products in sex by erogenizing every contour of every bottle or cigarette.
nine-inch-nails~heresy: In an interview with U. Magazine published April 1994, Reznor spoke about the meaning behind the lyrics of this song: “I was trying to explore some of the paranoia I have as a sexually active person in the age of AIDS,” he says of “Heresy.” “I guess I feel cheated for not growing up in a more liberated era.
the-dillinger-escape-plan~milk-lizard: One of The Dillinger Escape Plan’s most popular songs, “Milk Lizard” is a mixture of hardcore punk with jazz elements. Released in 2007, along with the critically acclaimed Ire Works, it discusses a toxic relationship.
nine-inch-nails~survivalism: “Survivalism” is the third track and the first single from the 2007 album Year Zero. Survivalism is the belief that one must be prepared to survive a major catastrophe by stocking up on food and weapons.
limp-bizkit~my-way: One of two songs from Chocolate Starfish and the Hot Dog Flavored Water to crossover into pop success on the Hot 100, “My Way” samples the classic Eric B.
a-perfect-circle~the-outsider: “The Outsider” was the second single from APC’s second album Thirteenth Step. It reached the top 5 on both the Mainstream and Alternative Rock Charts, and crossed over onto the US Pop Chart as a minor hit, reaching #79 in May 2004.
combichrist~never-surrender: Never Surrender was released on August 6, 2010 by the band CombieChrist. The lyrics appear to tell of a man who’s suffering from a drug addiction, likely amphetamines, and is casting away that part of himself.
linkin-park~figure-09: “Figure.09” stands as one of Meteora’s most visceral explorations of internal conflict and psychological fragmentation. The track’s aggressive instrumentation—marked by Brad Delson’s grinding guitar riffs and Joe Hahn’s turntable experimentation—mirrors its lyrical themes of identity erosion and toxic relationships.
limp-bizkit~my-generation: The 3rd track off Limp Bizkit’s 2000 Album “Chocolate Starfish and the Hot Dog Flavored Water”. In this song, Fred talks about generation x, his generation which at the time was criticised by the media for being violent and disoriented.
northlane~carbonized: Carbonized is the fourth track to Northlane’s sixth album Obsidian. It was released on March 23, 2022 along with its own accompanying music video. Vocalist Marcus Bridge reveals that the song is about sexual abusers and predators, and demands that they be held accountable for their actions.
machine-head~elegy: The song is clearly about humans taking advantage of the earth and the damage they can cause to the environment. They reference the earth as a she, the mother earth, and claim that since we are all her children, we need to stop killing her and ourselves.
a-day-to-remember~paranoia: The first song released from A Day to Remember’s Bad Vibrations talks about the feelings of Paranoia, how it affects one’s daily life, and disallows someone to trust anyone else.
nine-inch-nails~echoplex: This song is Reznor’s way of questioning his own relevance in the modern era of music.
pro8l3m~2-17: ZNAJDZIECIE NAS RÓWNIEŻ NA INSTAGRAMIE! Rap Genius dla Początkujących? Jak korzystać z Rap Genius? Jak tworzyć własne adnotacje?
bring-me-the-horizon~antivist: “Antivist” is a sarcastic and angry track from BMTH’s Sempiternal. It talks against “slackvists,” this is why the song’s called “antivist.” The band’s most political song.
slayer~new-faith: New Faith by Slayer from ‘God Hates Us All’ The lyrics talk about the horror of the revelation and say that Satan will give you a life of immortal pleasures like you never knew if you’ll worship the Devil.
nine-inch-nails~closer: “Closer” is one of Nine Inch Nails‘ more famous songs, and was released as Halo 9 (with different US and UK versions), the second single from their second studio album The Downward Spiral.
kyuss~one-inch-man: The only single off of Kyuss’s final album, …And the Circus Leaves Town. Singer John Garcia said this was one of his favorite songs that he wrote as it related to him on a more personal level than other Kyuss songs.
nine-inch-nails~every-day-is-exactly-the: “Every Day Is Exactly the Same” was the third of the three successful singles released from With Teeth, Nine Inch Nails' fourth studio album. The song reached the number one spot on Billboard’s Hot Modern Rock Tracks chart and was nominated for Best Hard Rock Performance at the 2007 Grammy Awards, but lost to “Woman” by Wolfmother.
type-o-negative~christian-woman: The second single from the album, which helped push Bloody Kisses to gold and eventually platinum status. The song’s lyrical themes touch on religious allegory (i.e.
nine-inch-nails~last: This song was featured in the film Cabin in The Woods, as well as being DLC for the video game series Rock Band. Despite being on the 1992 release Broken, “Last” was not played live until February 10, 2007, in Lisbon, Portugal, the first show of the Performance 2007 tour.
bloodhound-gang~foxtrot-uniform-charlie-: The lead single off of 2005’s Hefty Fine is riddled with sexual innuendo. Even the song’s title uses the NATO phonetic alphabet to spell out the word ‘fuck’.
bring-me-the-horizon~shadow-moses: “Shadow Moses” is the lead single from Bring Me The Horizon’s fourth album Sempiternal. Also, six years after the release of 2013’s “Shadow Moses,” Bring Me The Horizon entered in the soundtrack of Hideo Kojima’s DEATH STRANDING, a 2019 game from the same creator of Metal Gear.
northlane~eclipse: “Eclipse” is the seventh song off of Northlane’s album “Alien.” It was released as the fourth single on 29 July, 2019, along side Vultures, Bloodline and Talking Heads.
northlane~talking-heads: “Talking Heads” is the fourth track off of Northlane’s 2019 Album “Alien”. It was played live at many of their shows, though it was not released till 5 June 2019 as a single for the album, shortly after “Bloodline.”
nine-inch-nails~the-idea-of-you: Featuring Dave Grohl of Nirvana and Foo Fighers fame on the drums, “The Idea of You” alternates between whispering verses and a shouty and echoey chorus.
massive-attack~girl-i-love-you: Original working title was the inexplicably named “16 Seeter”, when the song debuted live in 2007.
nine-inch-nails~mr-self-destruct: The opener of The Downward Spiral, Nine Inch Nails' second studio album. Its loud and abrasive verses and choruses as well as the contrast in the quiet bridge and use of samples combine to make a comprehensive introduction to the sonic characteristics of the entire album.
nine-inch-nails~terrible-lie: “Terrible Lie” is the second track on Nine Inch Nails‘ first album Pretty Hate Machine and still a staple at their live shows, being the first track on both Closure and And All That Could Have Been live video-albums.
celldweller~switchback: “Switchback” is the second song off the self-titled debut album.
a-pale-horse-named-death~dmslt: The title “DMSLT” stands for “Doesn’t Make Sense Living Today.” A music video for this song was released on YouTube on September 2nd, 2013, and is currently the only music video the band has ever officially released.
linkin-park~by-myslf-josh-abraham-and-mi: “By_Myslf” is a remix of “By Myself”, originally on Linkin Park’s debut Hybrid Theory, and is the seventeenth track off Linkin Park’s remix album Reanimation.
grimes~kill-v-maim: “Kill V. Maim” is Grimes‘ favorite track off of her fourth LP, Art Angels. She told Q Magazine: “Kill V. Maim” is written from the perspective of Al Pacino in The Godfather Pt II.
type-o-negative~life-is-killing-me: Title track and eighth song on the 2003 album Life Is Killing Me is written from the point of view of a terminal patient in a hospital setting, tired of being kept alive against their will despite their suffering.
nine-inch-nails~we-re-in-this-together: “We’re in This Together” was released as a triple single, Halo 15. It took two weeks for the band to record and compile the diverse sounds of the song together.
deadmau5~sofi-needs-a-ladder-ft-sofi: “SOFI Needs a Ladder” is the second track on deadmau5’s fifth studio album. SOFI in this song depicts the effect that this relationship has had on her as well as her extreme distaste for this man who has screwed her over, presumably by cheating.
nine-inch-nails~ruiner: Trent Reznor stated it was “the hardest song to write” for the record in a 1994 interview. “I still don’t know if I got it right,” he said. “I have such a bad vibe from that song now – from it sucking in so many different ways.
northlane~4d: “4D” is the third song off of Northlane’s 2019 album, “Alien”. The band recorded a live version on 31 October 2019 with Triple J’s “Like a Version” segment.
rammstein~wiener-blut: Der Inhalt des Stückes „Wiener Blut“ basiert auf dem Fall Josef Fritzl, bei dem ein Vater seine Tochter 24 Jahre lang im Keller gefangen hielt und mit ihr sieben Kinder zeugte.
gorillaz~clint-eastwood: “Clint Eastwood” is the phenomenal debut single from “virtual band” Gorillaz, dropped in 2001. The funky aesthetic of the video and the song’s menacing, mellow beat made it one of the singles to bump in that year.
hollywood-undead~we-are: We Are is the first single by Hollywood Undead off of their third album, Notes from the Underground. The song’s music video was directed by Shawn “Clown” Crahan, drummer and founding member of the heavy metal band Slipknot.
rammstein~rosenrot: Rosenrot is a track from the 2005 album of the same name. The track was originally intended for the 2004 album Reise, Reise, though was replaced with Mein Teil shortly before release.
limp-bizkit~eat-you-alive: The lead off single from the record, it began their slow decline from commercial success, although it did manage to chart in 18 countries. The video features Fred Durst kidnapping a girl and screaming at her.
machine-head~davidian: This song is about the Waco Siege in 1993 at Waco, Texas. A branch of Davidians at a compound were raided by ATF agents but an gun battle erupted that resulted in the death of 4 government agents and 6 Davidians.
machine-head~now-i-lay-thee-down: Now I Lay Thee Down is a Romeo and Juliet-style story. It tells the tragic tale of a man who wants to die, and his girlfriend helps him commit suicide.
nine-inch-nails~the-day-the-world-went-a: Trent Reznor has not explicitly said so, but this song is rumored to be about his reaction to the death of his grandma, Clara, which happened before the recording of The Fragile.
nine-inch-nails~god-given: A slowed-down version of the song’s drum pattern seems to be used again on “Zero-Sum”. The pattern is also very similar to that of “My Violent Heart”.
nine-inch-nails~suck: “Suck” was originally written and performed by Pigface, a band lead by Martin Atkins and featuring Trent Reznor as well as many other notable industrial musicians.
strapping-young-lad~s-y-l: This song is about an audibly pissed-off Devin Townsend ranting about the music industry and his brief stint with Steve Vai’s band.
nine-inch-nails~the-line-begins-to-blur: “The Line Begins to Blur” is track 11 of 13 on With Teeth. The last three songs on With Teeth seem to describe the protagonist’s views of the world and the way things are going in it, i.e., the way he would like things to be going.
machine-head~slanderous: “Slanderous” deals with hate that still exists throughout society, the song has too many slurs. Marked [E] On iTunes.
nine-inch-nails~branches-bones: The opening song of Not the Actual Events, the abrasive return of Nine Inch Nails' chaos following 2013’s sobering Hesitation Marks. There has been speculation that the lyrics of this song are based on, or at least inspired by, the diary of a schizophrenic.
nine-inch-nails~beside-you-in-time: “Beside You in Time” starts quietly with a dark, uneasy drone fading in from the end of “The Line Begins to Blur”, which remains prominently throughout the entirety of the song and swells in volume in time with the simple drum loop.
porcupine-tree~sleep-together: The song has a strong vibe of suicide and ones purpose not being fulfilled. “Sleep” could be in the sense of putting to sleep (euthanasia) or sleep with the fishs.
bring-me-the-horizon~the-house-of-wolves: An attack on organized religion and the idea of a “deity.” Bring Me The Horizon asks for a reason to believe with “The House of Wolves.” In a track-by-track interview with MetalFuckingRocks, vocalist Oli Sykes explained: Lyrically, it’s all about religion.
nine-inch-nails~right-where-it-belongs: “Right Where It Belongs” is the 13th track on 2005’s With Teeth. Preview copies for media had it labeled “Cages,” though it’s unknown whether that was a working title or just an anti-piracy measure.
nine-inch-nails~sin: “Sin”, also known as Halo 4, is the third single from the album Pretty Hate Machine. Released in October 1990, the song peaked at #35 in the UK Singles Chart.
nine-inch-nails~capital-g: “Capital G” is about a politician, most likely about the fictional President of the United States of America within the plot of Year Zero, and his greed and sense of self preservation.
filter~the-best-things: This is the fifth track and third single off of Filter’s 1999 release “Title Of Record”
nine-inch-nails~the-beginning-of-the-end: “The Beginning of the End” is the second track on the 2007 release Year Zero. Like “HYPERPOWER!” preceding it, the song builds throughout the track, beginning with the drum loop, then guitar and vocals.
rammstein~zwitter: This song is about someone having both male and female genitalia. Zwitter means “hermaphrodite”. The song suggest the person could be in a relationship with themselves and even go as far as to impregnate or have sex with themselves.
make-them-suffer~hollowed-heart: “Hollowed Heart” confronts sexual violence, and the effects this has on victims. It is both an attack on those who would perpetrate such violence and a song of solidarity to those who have survived and suffer the effects to live another day.
slayer~cult: Cult was written by guitarist King as a comment on both religion and America, which he describes as “the biggest cult in the world”
megadeth~holy-wars-the-punishment-due: “Holy Wars… The Punishment Due” is the opening track off Megadeth’s fourth studio album Rust in Peace. The song consists of two different parts. The track’s first half, “Holy Wars,” is written about the religious conflict in Northern Ireland, which started in 1960, but has its roots as far back as the 17th Century.
bloodhound-gang~ralph-wiggum: Every line in the song is a direct quote from longtime running cartoon The Simpsons, centered around Ralph Wiggum, the dim-witted son of Chief Clancy Wiggum.
limp-bizkit~why-try: The twelfth track off of Limp Bizkit’s 2011 album, Gold Cobra. This song also appears on the soundtrack for Real Steel.
serj-tankian~cornucopia: This song tells us about how we destroyed the earth and it counterattacks us with natural disasters. It opens the door for the great environmental message of “Harakiri” from the great Serj Tankian.
bring-me-the-horizon~parasite-eve: “Parasite Eve,” the second single from POST HUMAN: SURVIVAL HORROR, was recorded during the 2020’s quarantine and, although the inspiration for the track didn’t come from the COVID-19 pandemic, there are a lot of references to it.
architects~these-colours-don-t-run-feat-: “These Colours Don’t Run” is another politically charged song by the British band Architects.
deftones~prayers-triangles: The single Prayers/Triangles is the opening track to Deftones' eighth studio album, Gore. Released on February 4th, 2016, it gives fans a taste of the full album which is scheduled for an April 8th release.
nine-inch-nails~sunspots: Driven throughout by consistent drum and bass parts, Reznor’s layered vocals and muted guitars soon join. The higher, melodic verses are followed by lower pitched vocals in the pre-choruses, then higher again as the choruses are sung in falsetto.
system-of-a-down~p-l-u-c-k: All the members in System are of Armenian descent and outspoken about their views of the Armenian Genocide of 1915. This song is a giant middle finger to the Turkish government, as well as a hope that the genocide would be officially recognized by the American government.
nine-inch-nails~you-know-what-you-are: Beginning immediately with Dave Grohl’s quick drumming, the song is differentiated as the only studio NIN song featuring prominent double bass technique.
queens-of-the-stone-age~no-one-knows: “No One Knows” lyrically deal with hallucinatory drugs, narcotics and prescription drugs, and the positive/negative sides to them, as well as all the unknown effects.
queens-of-the-stone-age~avon: A rough cut of the song named “Nova” was released through Josh Homme’s Desert Sessions vol. 3 & 4. “Avon” is nova spelled backwards.
megadeth~hangar-18: Off Megadeth’s Rust in Peace album, “Hangar 18” is about the story of an Air Force base in Dayton, Ohio where some people believe the alien bodies were taken when a UFO supposedly crashed in Roswell, New Mexico in 1947.
nine-inch-nails~getting-smaller: The lyrics may address Trent Reznor coming back from his addictions as a humbled person. According to at least one media promo listening, the working title for the song was slightly longer, appearing as “Getting Smaller Every Day.”
a-day-to-remember~bad-vibrations: A Day To Remember releases their title track to their 2016 release. When Paranoia was released Jeremy McKinnon told Zane Lowe on Beats 1 Radio (via Apple Music) that they were not releasing an album and that they were simply “casually making music” and that they decided to drop a random song as they are no longer bound to a record label.
cage-the-elephant~aberdeen: The song was originally named Maybelline but was later changed to Aberdeen because Cage the Elephant’s lead singer Matt Shultz couldn’t stop singing the city’s name while rehearsing in Aberdeen, Scotland.
nine-inch-nails~1-000-000: The song is similar to “Wish” in that it opens the record following a short instrumental (“999,999” like “Wish” following “Pinion”) and is at nearly the same tempo (“Wish” is at 135 BPM).
trivium~in-waves: In Waves was the first single of Orlando metal band Trivium’s fifth record ofthe same name. It was released on May 21st 2011. In Waves was the first single to feature Nick Augusto on drums.
nine-inch-nails~hurt: “Hurt” is the last song on Nine Inch Nails' most popular album, The Downward Spiral, which was released in 1994. The track is riddled with fuzz, distortion, and choppy audio while Trent Reznor’s voice softly flows over the accumulating noise.
the-prodigy~wall-of-death: A powerful album closer from “The Day is my Enemy”. A “wall of death” might have multiple meanings – it’s a name of a stunt show consisting of a motorcyclist driving inside a wooden cylinder (which forms a wall).
nine-inch-nails~burn: “Burn” is a song initially written for the Natural Born Killers Soundtrack, which was produced by Trent Reznor. It is a very intense track containing lyrics with anti-social and angry themes.
cradle-of-filth~temptation: The overall response to Thornography has been favorable by the metalheads, both American stateside, and in the international markets.

machine-head~locust: Locust was written collaboratively between vocalist and guitarist Robb Flynn and guitarist Phil Demmel, with Demmel originally coming up with the locust concept for the song and the album.
muse~apocalypse-please: The opening (although technically second) track of Absolution. The jarring, explosive piano chords that define much of the song’s melody evoke feelings of dread and – as the singer puts it – a sense of urgency.
dream-theater~misunderstood: Written by guitarist John Petrucci, “Misunderstood” speaks of the difficulties of living with mental illness, interpersonal and emotional struggles. As for the tone, “Misunderstood” draws on a melodic sound.
serj-tankian~butterfly: In this song, Serj Tankian speaks up regarding environmental problems with spiritual undertones. “Butterfly” is a popular symbolism of metamorphosis when a caterpillar overtime turns into a butterfly.
megadeth~never-walk-alone-a-call-to-arms: This song is about how someone will always be there to help you, even if you don’t realise. The lyrics were probably inspired by Mustaine’s conversion to Christianity after he had unexpectedly fully recovered from an arm injury.
bring-me-the-horizon~can-you-feel-my-hea: “Can You Feel My Heart” is the opening track and third single (fourth in the US) from Bring Me The Horizon’s fourth studio album Sempiternal. The song leads with Oliver Sykes’ depression.
nine-inch-nails~that-s-what-i-get: “That’s What I Get” is a fairly straightforward song about infidelity, notable for its use of steel drum synth in the bass line that begins the song.
pezet~rock-n-roll: Rap Genius dla Początkujących? Jak korzystać z Rap Genius? Jak tworzyć własne wyjaśnienia?
system-of-a-down~science: This song details the negative effects science has had upon our society. While it is more often than not hailed in a positive ‘omniscient’ light, Serj believes it has robbed us of the most powerful part of our existence.
grimes~you-ll-miss-me-when-i-m-not-aroun: Originally titled Last Call, You’ll miss me when I’m not around is the 8th track in Miss Anthropocene, corresponding to the Demon of Suicide.
pixies~where-is-my-mind: Black Francis says that “Where Is My Mind?” was written during the time he was attending the University of Massachusetts Amherst, and was inspired by the experience of having a small fish chase him while he was snorkeling in the Caribbean.
pro8l3m~outro: ZNAJDZIECIE NAS RÓWNIEŻ NA INSTAGRAMIE! Rap Genius dla Początkujących? Jak korzystać z Rap Genius? Jak tworzyć własne wyjaśnienia?
serj-tankian~praise-the-lord-and-pass-th: Praise the Lord and Pass the Ammunition discusses hypocrisy among religious people as it comes to conflict; people praising (the lord) then going and violating their religion by performing deeds of war and conflict.
gorillaz~dirty-harry: Gorillaz craft a single that examines war and soldiers, reminiscent of Jedi Mind Tricks' “Uncommon Valor” in its first-person look at the battlefield.
rammstein~te-quiero-puta: Deutsch: Dieses Lied handelt von einem unbekannten Protagonisten, der Sex mit einer Prostituierten hat, was ihm sehr gut gefällt. Das Lied ist komplett auf Spanisch und enthält keinerlei deutsche oder englische Elemente, was es für Rammstein einzigartig macht.
megadeth~liar: “Liar” is the seventh track off Megadeth’s third album So Far, So Good… So What! (1988), and possibly one of the first diss tracks of thrash metal. It was written by founder Dave Mustaine and bassist David Ellefson.
nightwish~the-poet-and-the-pendulum: This song is one of many explicitly acknowledged self-portraits or self-references by Nightwish frontman, Tuomas Holopainen. The song reflects Tuomas' feelings about all the drama in the band that happened in the year, 2005, as well as a dark period filled with depression and anxiety he experienced in 2006.
filter~stuck-in-here: This is the sixth track of Filter’s 1995 debut album “Short Bus”
muse~sober: About Alcoholism. The song references several different brands of whiskey, namely Royal Canadian, Wild Turkey, Arbourler, and Jameson. A flash based video was made for this song which features Bellamy.
crystal-castles~empathy: The 6th song from the 2nd Crystal Castles album juxtaposes a life cycle of a butterfly or just insects in general with the process of deterioration of a human mind by the modern society, the theme that is also adressed in the https://genius.com/Crystal-castles-celestica-lyrics and is central to the 4th Crystal Castles album, Amnesty.
megadeth~reckoning-day: This song is about retribution, about being paid back for the evil deeds that you have done in your life. A day of reckoning is a day of settlement and resolution.
megadeth~poison-was-the-cure: This song is a reflection of Dave Mustaine’s former heroin addiction and about the relationship he was in with a fellow addict. The title is analogous to methadone, a narcotic often used in treating heroin addiction.
porcupine-tree~way-out-of-here: An inspiration for this song was the story of two Wisconsin girls who apparently killed themselves in a suicide pact. Arielle Daniel, 17, and Heather Bates, 14, were struck by an Amtrak train on November 12, 2005 in what was ruled a suicide, but was possibly an accident.
tool~the-pot: “The Pot” is meticulously written to criticize hypocrisy, mainly targeting the church (Christianity) and the government. This song comes on the heels of the titular “10,000 Days”, a song in which Maynard calls out hypocrisy in the congregations of the church his mother attended.
slipknot~everything-ends: It’s about the last time I tried to commit suicide, and more to the point, the first day I figured out what life was all about. It’s about the last day in a life I don’t want to remember and about the first day in a life that I am living right now.
nine-inch-nails~came-back-haunted: “Came Back Haunted” was the only commercial single released from Nine Inch Nails' eighth studio album Hesitation Marks (aka Halo 28). To date, it is the only commercially released NIN single not to have a Halo number.
slipknot~the-blister-exists: It’s about how everything that they have said in their songs does exist and is part of the band’s inner workings.
trash-boat~vertigo: It’s such a butt-rock song, isn’t it? Vertigo’s one of the songs we pretty much wrote in the studio. We were channeling that Nirvana, Oasis energy where a song doesn’t necessarily need to completely make sense.
system-of-a-down~toxicity: “Toxicity” is one of System of a Down’s biggest hits, the song reached No. 70 on Billboard Hot 100 and No. 3 on Mainstream Rock chart. During a performance at the 2005 Download Festival, Daron Malakian said that the song was about Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD), a psychological condition that interferes with an individual’s executive functions.
system-of-a-down~cigaro: “Cigaro” details the pointless and seemingly endless obsession of ‘Boys and their Toys’, whether it be the explicit concept of penis size or the size of their guns, men are always trying to prove their superiority over one another.
pearl-jam~even-flow: “Even Flow” is the second single from Pearl Jam’s inaugural album Ten. It is a narration of the life of a homeless person. It is a favorite for many, including Matt Cameron (who was Soundgarden’s drummer at the time).
korn~got-the-life: “Got The Life” was released as a promotional single for their third studio album. Though never actually making much of a radio buzz until after the band’s success, the music video was the first video ever to be retired on MTV’s “TRL”, which was a much bigger deal in the 90s.
northlane~jinn: Jinn is the 6th Track off of Northlane’s 5th album, Alien. Jinn was described as sounding similar to the band’s prior releases and as being “a turning point moment; of breaking through barriers, of refusing generational pain.”
pendulum~crush: It’s the fourth single from Australian drum and bass band Pendulum released from their third studio album, Immersion. Rob Swire said in an interview that it was about a break up with his ex-girlfriend.
limp-bizkit~90-2-10: The eleventh track off of Limp Bizkit’s 2011 album, Gold Cobra.
limp-bizkit~douche-bag: “Douche Bag” is a song by Limp Bizkit from their album Gold Cobra. The song is a threat to a person the band’s frontman Fred Durst isn’t exactly a big fan of.
nightwish~storytime: Storyime is the lead single of Finnish symphonic metal band Nightwish’s seventh album, Imaginaerum (2011). The song tells the story of an old man on his deathbed recalling a story to a young person.
machine-head~vim: A song with a similar meaning to Imperium. You have the power to change your own life and you can make a choice to be happy. You can be yourself and ignore the people who tell you what to do or bring you down.
queens-of-the-stone-age~run-pig-run: “Run, Pig, Run” is the eleventh and last track on the regular version of “Era Vulgaris”.
deftones~royal: The song Royal works with the imagery of a priestess coming from up high, the space even, who is prompted by the speaker to contact him and thus with humankind.
queens-of-the-stone-age~my-god-is-the-su: “My God is the Sun” was released as the lead single off of … Like Clockwork.
hilltop-hoods~what-a-great-night: As the third single from Hilltop Hoods' fourth album, What a Great Night pays tribute to endless nights of drinking at the bars and clubs of Adelaide.

muse~plug-in-baby: Again, that’s about the path evolution can take like ‘New Born’. There’s the good side and the bad side; abandoning all individuality, becoming a collective whole via cables, and genetically engineering bodies that can exist out in space, or the loss of individualism.
the-smiths~how-soon-is-now-2011-remaster: Sire Records chief Seymour Stein called it “the ‘Stairway to Heaven’ of the Eighties”, while co-writer Johnny Marr described it as “possibly our most enduring record.
slipknot~duality: “Duality” was the 1st single from Slipknot’s 3rd album, Vol 3: The Subliminal Verses, and was released on May 25, 2004. It was produced by Rick Rubin.
black-light-burns~coward: Track number 4 on industrial/alternative rock band, Black Light Burns features guest vocals from Sonny Moore (better known as the dubstep artist, Skrillex).
nine-inch-nails~find-my-way: Downtempo, atmospheric and contemplative, the song prominently features a beat loop that is constantly augmented with echoes throughout, reminiscent of trip hop beat programming.
gorillaz~tranz: “Tranz” (pronounced Trance) debuted—alongside three others—on June 1, 2018, at the Rock im Park festival in Nürnberg. It is the second-listed track on Gorillaz’s sixth studio album, The Now Now, which has since released on June 29, 2018.
slayer~raining-blood: One of Slayer’s signature songs both concludes and gives its name to their most acclaimed album. Writers Kerry King and the late Jeff Hanneman have said it’s one of their favorite songs to play live, and it has been an almost permanent fixture in their live set lists for more than 30 years.
machine-head~descend-the-shades-of-night: About death and the inevitability of death

dream-theater~these-walls: “These Walls” is a song in which the narrator is incredibly shy. He want’s to reach out, but he’s trapped within the “walls.” The walls in question could be anxiety that keeps him from what he wants.
nevermore~inside-four-walls: From the liner notes of The Politics of Ecstasy: “A young man named Christian Martenson is serving a 10 year prison term in the state of Arizona. He was set up by an undercover DEA agent over a two year period while following the Grateful Dead.

machine-head~night-of-long-knives: The song is about Charles Manson’s murders.
megadeth~washington-is-next: This song is about how the government is corrupt and will lead the U.S.A. to its downfall. The government tries to keep its population under control by keeping them stupid and destroying the family structure.
muse~citizen-erased: This song has a much deeper and darker meaning than what it looks like.
muse~supermassive-black-hole: The first single from Muse’s 2006 album Black Holes and Revelations. It’s kind of “dancy”-sounding and has one B-Side, “Crying Shame”. linkin-park~nobody-s-listening: “Nobody’s Listening” stands as the eleventh track on Linkin Park’s sophomore album, Meteora. The song is distinguished by its hip-hop-centered vibe and the distinctive Japanese flute loop that forms its backbone.
system-of-a-down~jet-pilot: “Jet Pilot” is one of those songs that nobody really knows the meaning behind—neither Serj Tankian, maybe.
pro8l3m~prequel: Parę słów od producenta:Ten bit powstawał dłuuugo, bo miałem okazję puścić jakiś fragment na szkole dźwięku Red Bulla w październiku 2015. W miksie przesterowaliśmy delikatnie bębny żeby nie było zbyt sterylnie.
kendrick-lamar~the-blacker-the-berry: Released via Empire star Taraji P. Henson’s twitter, this comes the day after Kendrick won Best Rap Song & Performance for “i” at the 2015 Grammy Awards.

type-o-negative~a-dish-best-served-coldl: “…A Dish Best Served Coldly” is a song by Type O Negative, included on their sixth album Life Is Killing Me, released in 2003. This song features the acoustic guitar and choirs by Kenny Hickey, as well as what sounds like metal cooking pots embedded in it.

the-prodigy~smack-my-bitch-up: As can be expected with a song called “Smack My Bitch Up”, this song caused an uproar as it appeared to promote violence against women. refused~elektra: the first single, and the opening track, of the album Freedom. the single was released in april 2015, just before their first show since the reunion tour.
wargasm-uk~salma-hayek: “Salma Hayek” is an energetic, nu-metal track from WARGASM, focused around the actress Salma Hayek as she appears in the 1996 film From Dusk ‘Til Dawn.
northlane~echo-chamber: Echo Chamber is the third track to Northlane’s sixth album Obsidian. It was released on November 10, 2021 along with its own accompanying music video.
metallica~that-was-just-your-life: “That Was Just Your Life” opens Death Magnetic to the sound of thrash metal Metallica anno 2008. The song is about an ex-soldier who suffers from PTSD and describes how the flashbacks from the battlefield feel and how they interfere with his/her everyday life.

rammstein~pussy: Controversial song that’s partly responsible for their album getting banned from display in their own country, Germany. In this song, Till takes the role of a sex tourist in Germany, that doesn’t really know the culture, is really only here for the legalized prostitution.
type-o-negative~stay-out-of-my-dreams: This song was previously unreleased, it is one of the 3 new compositions on this compilation album.
linkin-park~qwerty-live: Produced by Rick Rubin, the man who helped the band during “Minutes To Midnight”, “A Thousand Suns” and “Living Things”. “Qwerty” is a B-side to Minutes to Midnight, containing Chester Benningtons powerful screaming and Mike Shinodas dark rhymes.
celldweller~ghosts-feat-tom-salta: Celldweller talks about the fallen ones he used to know.
nightwish~bye-bye-beautiful: “Bye Bye Beautiful” is the second track, and fourth single from Nightwish’s sixth album, Dark Passion Play. The song is about Nightwish’s first former vocalist, Tarja Turunen, who was dismissed via an open letter in October, 2005.
the-prodigy~nasty: Receiving mixed critical acclaim and remixed by the likes of Zinc and Feed Me (Spor), “Nasty” combines signature Prodigy vocals with a twisted instrumental and a video where fox hunting goes wrong.
slayer~piece-by-piece: Track 2 On Slayer’s Infamous Reign In Blood, This Song Is Most Likely Describing A Murderer/Cannibal Killing And Butchering His Victim Before Consuming Them.
megadeth~dawn-patrol: This song is a short and somber warning about the possibility of a nuclear holocaust; more specifically, what life would be like after the incident. Humans would be forced to live underground because the atmosphere would be so contaminated with radioactive pollution left over from the nuclear weapons.
kyuss~catamaran: “Catamaran” was originally written and recorded by “Yawning Man,” Alfredo Hernandez’s (Brant Bjork’s replacement) previous band.
the-rolling-stones~gimme-shelter: “Gimme Shelter” is the opening track of the Rolling Stones’s 1969 album Let It Bleed (where it was actually spelled “Gimmie Shelter”, but the more accurate spelling was adopted afterwards).
nine-inch-nails~the-way-out-is-through: The 1st track of the “Right” side of Halo 14. It is one of the more abstract and non-traditional songs on the album, with its structure based around a drawn-out crescendo.
deftones~tempest: “Tempest” is the second single off of Deftones' Koi No Yokan. The lyrics suggest that the song revolves around the idea of the end of the world. Considering the song was released in 2012, it’s safe to assume it’s referring to the end of the Mayan Calendar in particular.
kendrick-lamar~dna: On “DNA.,” Kendrick Lamar adopts multiple viewpoints; celebrating, critiquing, and exploring his black heritage and culture. In the music video, Kendrick and actor Don Cheadle appear to trade bars, engaging in an argument using the song’s lyrics during an interrogation scene.
magdalena-bay~halfway: The midpoint of the album, aptly named “Halfway”. With the looming presence of the concept of time as a loop, sparkled throughout the album, Magdalena Bay went a step further beyond naming the album’s opener “The End” and the closer “The Beginning”: they named the middle as “Halfway”.
muse~the-small-print: A song about selling your soul to the devil, hence the dark, evil lyrics. Some of the vocals in the verses have evil-sounding effects on them too. periphery~ji: “Ji” is a gender-neutral honorific in many South Asian languages. It is put before a name to indicate that the speaker has respect for that person/thing.
polaris~landmine: This is what band’s drummer Daniel Furnari says about song in track-by-track on AltPress: I think there comes a time for a lot of us in our early adulthood where we finally realize the world doesn’t exist to serve us as individuals and never did.
alice-glass~forgiveness: “Forgiveness. This song is about rejecting the idea of forgiveness. Forgiveness isn’t always a moral act, the way some religions portray it. Sometimes forgiveness can be exploitative or even predatory, especially when people use it as a means to guilt someone rather than heal them.
slipknot~three-nil: Three Nil is a term used for an embarrassing defeat. Three Nil is 3-0. This song is also about being put on display in your shame: “I’m sick of being the butt of a cosmic joke and I don’t get the punchline.

megadeth~peace-sells: This song is the Mustaine’s way of dispelling many of the stereotypes about the band and heavy metal fans in general. More specifically, that they are lazy, anti-government, and anti-religion, which Mustaine says are mostly untrue.
dream-theater~the-dark-eternal-night: In the video “Chaos in Progress”, John Petrucci simply said that this song is about a monster. The Dark Eternal Night is about a pharaoh who has returned after dormancy as a monster to haunt a town.
rammstein~der-meister: Der Meister beschreibt die Apokalypse im Christentum.
the-prodigy~destroy: Gritty instrumentals infused angry vocals, a classic Prodigy track.
deadmau5~seeya: The second single off of Deadmau5’s while(1˂2). The song featuring vocals from The Material’s Colleen D'Agostino. It describes the emotions of a sleeper invested in the world they have created in their dreams.
deftones~rapture: This song is about the confusion Chino faced with other members of the band over the recording sessions of this album
dream-theater~the-looking-glass: Written by guitarist John Petrucci, “The Looking Glass” is directed at young artists entering in the music who are mesmerised by fame and can’t seem to get enough of this, despite the compromise this puts on others.
serj-tankian~baby: Song 7 off of Serj Tankian’s debut solo album, Elect the Dead. This song isn’t just a love song on the surface, it actually has one large deeper meaning that can be traced back to Sigmund Freud’s ideologies of the human mind, where (in the video) the man represents id, the woman superego (or one’s conscious), and the book in which she transcribes ego.
exodus~good-day-to-die: The eleventh track on “Force of Habit” tells the story about a man who, despite having a seemingly normal and social life, still ends up dissatisfied and alone.
massive-attack~what-your-soul-sings: What Your Soul Sings' genesis came about from one of the very few leftover pieces of music composed during the 2000/2001 Lupine Howl period, which were mostly all scrapped.
the-prodigy~warrior-s-dance: The third single from the group’s fifth album. It was one of their most popular, peaking at #9 in the UK charts. The video features creatures made out of cigarette packets trashing a pub and causing a fire.
nine-inch-nails~into-the-void: “Into the Void” is one of several songs on The Fragile that shares a melody with one or more other songs. It is a sister-track to “La Mer”, also from The Fragile; both songs grew out of the same initial track and feature the same bass line and melody.
system-of-a-down~fuck-the-system: If the title of the song wasn’t clue enough; this song is just a big fuck you to the system. A lot of the band’s personal work goes toward preventing/spreading awareness of war and genocide.
bring-me-the-horizon~crooked-young: “Crooked Young” mainly talks about Oliver Sykes' ketamine rehabilitation. system-of-a-down~darts: “Why do old societies hold the pantheon of 12 gods to be true, while modern societies generally have one God?” – footnote from the booklet for the album

nine-inch-nails~zero-sum: The last song on Year Zero, “Zero Sum” describes what could be seen as either divine retribution or human-triggered apocalypse. The title is in reference to the economic or mathematical theory of a “zero-sum game”.
machine-head~all-falls-down: A metaphoric song about how fear conquers you.

rob-zombie~living-dead-girl: One of the best known solo hits by Rob Zombie, this song offers an homage to the B-Flicks and Cult Classics of the past. The music video to the song is a direct satire off of the classic Weimar film The Caibnet of Dr.
rammstein~ich-tu-dir-weh: “Ich tu dir weh” (“I hurt you” or “I do you woe”) is the second single from the 2009 album Liebe ist für alle da by German band Rammstein. Due to the sado-masochistic content of this song, the album was placed on the “index” maintained by the German Bundesprüfstelle für jugendgefährdende Medien.
limp-bizkit~introbra: “Introbra” is the intro track on Limp Bizkit’s 2011 album Gold Cobra. Its title combines the words “intro” and “cobra” for the truly exquisite “introbra.” Get it?
crystal-castles~wrath-of-god: The third track from Crystal Castles' third album “(III)”. The song is mainly composed of distorted, icy synths and a simple thumping kick drum beat with some ambient and noise sound effects added in.
